mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month trailing median.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income and consumption demand, triggering equity risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not negative in at least 60% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining nominal retail sales alongside elevated consumer price inflation compress corporate profit margins and erode purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month that neither proxy achieves in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce a negative rank correlation with 20-day returns (Rank IC > -0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-shock recovery periods signals rebounding manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where INDPRO is accelerating after a prior contraction.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy materially raises corporate borrowing costs and slows economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in nonfarm payrolls and residential construction starts signals broad labor market strength and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal of PAYEMS and HOUST jointly fails to outperform either univariate signal in predicting positive 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals contracting aggregate demand and falling corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters following a GDPC1 contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated nominal retail sales reflect resilient consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations in the top quintile of RSAFS.
